data.title: June PCE: Inflation Reaccelerates Despite Tepid Spending And Income Gains
data.description:
The June personal income and outlays report highlighted a concerning dynamic for policymakers: soft income and spending growth paired with firmer-than-expected inflation. Personal income rose just 0.3% MoM while personal consumption also increased 0.3% MoM, below expectations. Yet, both headline and core PCE inflation accelerated to 0.3% MoM, driven by a broad pickup in goods prices, likely reflecting early impacts from new tariffs. The data reinforces the Fed’s cautious stance, with short-term inflation gauges suggesting the disinflationary momentum of early 2025 has faded.

Income and Spending

Income continued to show a trend of gradual moderation in the final month of the second quarter after a bit of volatility in April and May. In June, personal income increased just 0.3% MoM, the weakest monthly increase since November 2024 when excluding the previous two months (which were impacted due to volatility in transfer receipts). Notably, this month saw just a 0.2% MoM increase in employee compensation with wages & salaries up just 0.1% MoM. This was the weakest MoM gain in compensation since a year ago, when the Fed judged there to be a noticeable uptick in labor market looseness.

While there was weakness in compensation growth, disposable income growth was also 0.3% MoM as personal transfer receipts increased 1.0% MoM and personal taxes only increased 0.3% MoM (the lowest increase since April 2024). An even more troubling view on income can be created by excluding personal transfer receipts and adjusting for inflation. That measure was down -0.2% MoM in June, matching the decline of -0.2% MoM in May. When looking at Q2 as a whole, real personal income (ex-current transfer receipts) was down -0.2% QoQ, the weakest since a -0.4% QoQ drop in Q2 2022.

Tame income growth appears to have tempered the gain in personal consumption expenditures. After no change in May, personal spending was up just 0.3% MoM, falling short of an expected 0.4% MoM increase. Spending growth across goods and services was pretty similar, with the former up 0.5% MoM and the latter up 0.3% MoM. The breakdown of spending shows that the largest increases in spending were in health care, housing, and energy goods. Discretionary categories like furnishings, recreational goods & services, and food services & accommodations all saw no growth to a small decline. A drop in spending on vehicles in June also weighed on spending. What is more troubling is that even though personal consumption growth was relatively tame, the weakness in personal income growth led to a -$8.5 billion decline in personal savings.

PCE Inflation

Even though spending and income growth rates were subdued in June, the PCE price indexes posted their largest monthly gains since February. The headline PCE index increased 0.3% MoM and 2.6% YoY in June, with the monthly rate in line with forecasts and the latter slightly ahead of the 2.5% YoY expected gain. A bounce back in energy was the main reason for the acceleration in the headline annual rate, with that subindex up 0.9% MoM. Food prices were also up a decent clip, rising 0.3% MoM. With PCE inflation back up to 2.6% YoY, the disinflation over the last three months from weaker energy prices has basically vanished.

The Fed’s preferred inflation measure, core PCE inflation, was 0.3% MoM and 2.8% YoY in June. Like the headline index, the monthly rate was in line with expectations, while the annual rate beat by 0.1 ppts. Driving core PCE prices higher was a 0.4% MoM increase in the goods segment, the largest since January of this year, reflecting the rise in goods inflation seen in the June CPI report. The goods rise was pretty even across durable (+0.5% MoM) and nondurables (+0.4% MoM), but the durable move was the second largest since September 2022. It’s hard not to see tariffs in this move, especially when spending growth on durables was nonexistent. In Q2 as a whole, durable goods prices increased 1.0% QoQ, the largest quarterly increase since Q1 2022. Outside of goods, the PCE services segment was up 0.2% MoM for the fourth straight month, not playing much of a major role in the overall core PCE index change.

Market Reaction

The June personal income and outlays report was a bit of a mixed bag. Income and spending growth were clearly soft, especially when looking at employee compensation and non-discretionary spending. However, both core and headline PCE annual inflation came in higher than expected, with there being evidence that tariffs are flowing into prices. The mixed report generated a mixed response in financial markets:

  • S&P futures had a volatile initial reaction but ultimately moved sideways into the market open. However, at open, the S&P 500 continued the rally from yesterday, rising about 0.6%. The Russell 2000, a bit more sensitive to interest rates, is not faring as well, falling about -0.5% at market open.
  • The move in interest rates was also a bit choppy after the release this morning. Rates immediately fell in the first 15 minutes but mostly snapped back higher. On the day, the yield curve is broadly lower by about 1 to 2 bps, but that may be more of a cooling off from the strong rise in rates following yesterday’s FOMC meeting.
  • The US dollar index has moved in a more clear direction since the release, rising from a slight loss in the morning to a gain of about 0.2% at market open. The stronger dollar is likely a symptom of Powell’s hawkishness yesterday, combined with some reversal momentum coming from the weak performance in the first two quarters of the year.

How is the Fed viewing today’s release? If we look just at the annual rate of core PCE inflation, the 2.8% YoY increase in June was the same as the increase in May, suggesting there might not be much new information to consider. However, if we factor in some shorter-term measures of inflation (6-month and 3-month annualized rates), we see that there has been a clear shift in June such that the disinflationary trend in the first half of the year has ended. I believe that we are seeing the beginning of the price impacts of tariffs. However, this isn’t necessarily new since CPI data showed this, and Powell pointed it out in his press conference yesterday.

As I mentioned yesterday, this early evidence of tariff-related inflationary pressure is going to keep the Fed leaning hawkish unless it can be confident that either price increases are transitory or the upside risks to inflation have dropped. We saw a sharp repricing in Fed fund futures markets yesterday, pointing towards increased odds of a pause in September. Following today’s personal income and outlays report, the probability has risen again, up to over 60% (from around 52% at the end of the previous day). The Fed will remain patient until the data demands it take action.

data.title: American Tower's Meltdown Unwarranted - Buy The Raised Guidance
data.description:
AMT's Multi-Year Telecom/ Data-Center Prospects Remain Promising

We previously covered American Tower Corporation (NYSE:AMT) in May 2025, discussing how the telecom/ data center REIT had benefited from its vertically integrated telecom and data center/ interconnection capabilities, as it delivered excellent top/ bottom-line growth.

Combined with the healthy balance sheet despite the elevated debt levels and the doubled down data-center ambitions, we had believed that the REIT remained well positioned to report robust numbers moving forward, resulting in our reiterated Buy rating then.

AMT 1Y Stock Price

Since then, AMT has already retested the YTD peaks of $220s/ $230s ranges by July 22, 2025, with a similar recovery from April 2025 bottoms also observed in the telecom/ data center REIT peers and the wider market.

Much of the tailwinds are attributed to the reaffirmed durability of the AI-related spending trends in the early Q2'25 earnings season, as Alphabet (GOOG) notably raised their FY2025 server-related capex guidance and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited (TSM), the world's leading foundry, raised their FY2025 revenue growth guidance.

These developments signal why the uncertain macroeconomic/ geopolitical environment and the rapidly developing tariff war have yet to put a dent into the ongoing AI boom indeed.

A similarly promising sentiment has been shared by Digital Realty Trust, Inc. (DLR) as well, with the consistently higher base rent per square foot and the growing backlog demonstrating the undeniable pent up demand for data center capacity.

In the telecom tower segment, Crown Castle Inc. (CCI) has also highlighted how the increased deployment of 5G, its clients' expanding subscriber base, and the data demand growth in the US have already driven higher leasing/ services activities and consequently, top/ bottom-line growth.

These reasons may also be why AMT has already raised their FY2025 total property revenue guidance to $10.2B at the midpoint (+0.7% YoY), adj EBITDA margins to 68.9% (+1.6 points YoY/ +5.9 from FY2019 levels of 63%), and AFFO per share to $10.55 (+5.9% YoY) in the recent FQ2'25 earnings call.

This is compared to the original guidance of $9.99B (-1.2% YoY), 68.8% (+1.5 points YoY), and $10.40 (+4.4% YoY) offered in the FQ4'24 earnings call, respectively.

The robust prospects have been notably observed in AMT's data center revenue growth by +13.5% YoY in FQ2'25, further aided by the excellent International Organic Tenant Billings Growth at +6.5% YoY, with these well balancing the sluggish UCAN regions at +3.7% YoY.

With the management expecting a similar data center growth profile at +13% YoY and International growth at +4.6% YoY in FY2025, it goes without saying that the market has over-reacted to the telecom/ data center REIT's FQ2'25 AFFO per share miss by $0.01 indeed, with the -6.5% correction post-earnings overly done.

While AMT's deteriorating balance sheet health to a net leverage ratio of 5.1x in FQ2'25 (compared to 5.0x in FQ1'25, 4.8x in FQ2'24, and 4.6x in FY2019) warrants close monitoring, these are partly attributed to higher capital expenditures for new lands/ towers and data center projects in the US/ EU.

Given the growing telecom backlog (application pipeline) by over +50% YoY, the ongoing 5G upgrading/ densification/ colocation cadence, and the multi-year data center backlog at over $80M (as per last reported in FQ4'24), we believe that the REIT's higher FY2025 capex plans at $1.5B (+7.1% YoY) are likely to be accretive in the long-term.

The Consensus Forward Estimates

These reasons may also be why the consensus still expects AMT to report an excellent top/ bottom-line growth at a CAGR of +4%/ +5.6% through FY2027, nearing that of the 5Y historical growth profile at +6%/ +5.9% albeit decelerating from the 10Y growth at +9.5%/ +8.8%, respectively.

AMT Valuations

Combined with the multi-year 5G and data-center related investment trends, we believe that AMT continues to trade attractively at FWD Price/ AFFO valuations of 21.11x, inline to the 5Y mean of 22.66x and the 10Y mean of 21.87x.

On the other hand, when comparing to its data center REIT peers, including:

  • Equinix, Inc. (EQIX) at FWD Price/ FFO valuations of 20.95x with the projected 3Y AFFO per share growth at +7.6%,
  • Iron Mountain Incorporated (IRM) at 19.53x/ +10.7%,
  • Digital Realty Trust, Inc. (DLR) at 26.63x/ +9.9%, respectively,

it appears that AMT's decelerating 3Y AFFO per share growth profile at +5.6% may have come at a relatively premium valuation.

This development implies that a moderate pullback to our Buy zones may be necessary for an improved return profile, one that we shall further discuss in the next segment.

So, Is AMT Stock A Buy, Sell, or Hold?

AMT 7Y Stock Price

For now, AMT has already lost much of its June 2025 gains, with the stock seemingly well defended at the intermediate support levels and the 50/ 100 day moving averages of $210s ranges.

AMT's Reversing Momentum

Even so, technical indicators imply a notable reversal momentum in AMT's upward recovery since the April/ May 2025 bottoms, worsened by the notably higher short interest volumes by +27.1% on a YoY basis, the rather greedy stock market sentiments by the time of writing, and the potentially weaker stock market season in August/ September 2025.

These developments imply further volatility in the near-term, depending on how the Q2'25 earnings season and the macroeconomic environment develop.

With the inflationary pressure rising in June 2025 and the Fed turning hawkish, it remains to be seen how H2'25 may develop as well, with the potential receding in market euphoria potentially sinking all ships, AMT included.

Otherwise, based on the management's raised FY2025 AFFO per share guidance to $10.55 (+5.9% YoY) and the FWD Price/ AFFO valuations of 21.11x, it is apparent that the correction has been a boon, with it triggering the AMT stock's inherent undervaluation to our estimated fair value estimates of $222.70.

A similar conclusion may also be observed against our long-term price target of $262.10, based on the consensus FY2027 AFFO per share estimates of $12.42, with the pullback contributing to an expanded upside potential of +22%.

As a result, we are cautiously reiterating our Buy rating for AMT, with the caveat that interested investors observe the stock price movement for a little longer before adding, since it remains to be seen if the $210s floor may hold in the near-term.

Otherwise, we may see a further retracement to the next support levels of $200s. Given the potential -6.8% downside from current levels, a moderate patience may be more prudent in the mean time.

data.title: Vanadium Miners News For The Month Of July 2025
data.description:
Welcome to the Vanadium miners news.

July saw Europe & China spot vanadium pentoxide prices lower and ferrovanadium prices lower for the past month.

Vanadium uses

Vanadium is traditionally used to harden steel. Chinese rebar standards are requiring more vanadium. Also, vanadium flow batteries [VRFBs] are becoming increasingly popular, especially for commercial energy storage, most notably in China. Vanadium pentoxide [V2O5] is used in VRFBs and ferrovanadium [FeV] is used in the steel industry.

Vanadium spot price history

As shown below, Europe V2O5 and China V2O5 prices were lower the last month, with Europe significantly lower.

Europe & China Vanadium Pentoxide [V2O5] Flake 98% one-year price chart - Europe = USD 4.19/lb, China = USD 3.95/lb

As of July 28, 2025, Argus Media reports the China Ferro-vanadium (80%V) FOB price at US$23.25/kg, slightly lower for the past month.

Europe and China Ferrovanadium [FeV] 80% one-year price chart - Europe = USD 23.35/kg, China = USD 23.25/kg

Vanadium demand versus supply

An April 2021 Wood Mackenzie report stated:

Global energy storage deployment surged a remarkable 62% in 2020, with 5 GW/9 GWh of new capacity added. This brought the total energy storage market to more than 27 GWh. Furthermore, we expect the global (energy storage) market to grow 27-fold by 2030.

ResearchAndMarkets forecasts the global VRFB market to grow from US$298m in 2023 to US$921m in 2028, at a CAGR of 20.5% (as of January 2023) (source)

Global VRFB forecast growth by region 2022-2031

Large scale global deployments of VRFB's are becoming more common - chart sourced as of April 2025 (source)

VRFB installations are rapidly appearing globally, notably in China (source)

Vanadium (8.5x) & Lithium (10x) demand forecast to surge the most under the IEA Net Zero Emission scenario (source)

Vanadium market news

An article we missed from last month gives a great summary of the vanadium market. On June 4 Investor.News reported:

Anthony Milewski on the double-edged demand of Vanadium. By 2033, vanadium redox flow batteries (VRFBs) are projected to account for 17% of global vanadium use — a x6 increase from just 3% in 2021. With steel still dominating vanadium demand (accounting for 94% of US consumption in 2023), this surge in battery use is expected to put significant pressure on supply. To meet this growing demand, global vanadium supply will need to increase by 6.9% annually between 2022-2030. However, 99% of global supply is concentrated in high risk regions: China (70%) and Russia (21%) and South Africa (8%). And most potential new sources of vanadium remain either un-financed or in early-stage exploration.

On June 30, Vanitec reported:

Construction begins on Ordos Gushanliang 3GW/12.8GWh Energy Storage Station Project, a major step in Inner Mongolia’s green energy transition...the project integrates multiple advanced energy storage technologies, including:

  • Lithium Iron Phosphate (LFP) batteries
  • Vanadium Flow Batteries (VFB)
  • Molten Salt Thermal Storage

On July 22, Vanitec reported:

Guangdong Zhongke Tianshang Energy to invest RMB 555 Million in Hybrid Energy Storage Project in Guangzhou...With a total installed capacity of 201MW/406MWh, the facility will comprise:

  • 200MW/400MWh of lithium iron phosphate batteries
  • 1MW/6MWh of vanadium flow batteries...

On July 24, Vanitec reported: "Hami’s first 100MW/400MWh Vanadium Flow Battery energy storage plant successfully completed."

Vanadium miner news

Vanadium producers

Glencore [LSX:GLEN] [HK:805] (OTCPK:GLCNF)

Glencore is a large vanadium producer, but vanadium production represents only a small portion of their revenue.

On July 7, Glencore announced:

2025/2026 share buy-back programme. Glencore plc (the "Company") announces the commencement of a programme to make market purchases of its ordinary shares (the "Shares") of an aggregate value of up to USD 1 billion (the "Programme"), subject to market conditions, with completion planned to be by the time of the release of our financial results for the 2025 year in February 2026. The Programme will be effected in accordance with the terms of the authority granted by shareholders at the 2025 AGM, to acquire no more than 1,809,287,297 Shares (of which 41,121,000 have already been purchased). The Programme's purpose is to reduce the capital of the Company. It is currently intended that any Shares purchased will be held in treasury.

AMG Critical Materials N.V. [NA:AMG] [GR:ADG] (OTCPK:AMVMF) ("AMG")

AMG Vanadium is a leading provider of products and services for the metals, manufacturing, refinery and petrochemical industries. You can read more about AMG Vanadium here.

No significant news for the month. Q2 earnings are due on July 30.

Largo Inc. [TSX:LGO] [GR:LR81] (LGORF)(NASDAQ:LGO)

Largo Inc. is a pure-play vanadium pentoxide producer from their Maracás Menchen Mine in Brazil as well as a producer of VRFBs.

On July 15, Largo Inc. announced: "Largo reports improved Q2 2025 vanadium production; Storion Energy to support 48 MWh Vanadium Flow Battery Deployment in Texas." Highlights include:

  • "V2O5 production of 2,256 tonnes (5.0 million lbs1) in Q2 2025 vs. 2,689 tonnes produced in Q2 2024 and 74% higher than 1,297 tonnes produced in Q1 2025.
  • Global recovery3 of 84.9% in Q2 2025, a 14% increase over the global recovery3 rate of 74.3% in Q2 2024 and 9% above the 77.8% averaged in Q1 2025.
  • Operational turnaround progressing as planned with improved mine access and mining rates, higher production and stronger recovery rates; The Company remains on track to achieve 2025 V₂O₅ production guidance.
  • V2O5 equivalent sales of 1,807 tonnes (inclusive of 123 tonnes of purchased material) in Q2 2025 vs. 1,841 equivalent tonnes sold (inclusive of 128 tonnes of purchased material) in Q2 2024.
  • Ilmenite concentrate production of 8,149 tonnes in Q2 2025 vs. 8,624 tonnes in Q2 2024 and 32% higher than the 6,162 tonnes produced in Q1 2025; Ilmenite sales of 6,024 tonnes vs. 12,261 tonnes in Q2 2024.
  • Storion Energy signs strategic supply agreement with TerraFlow Energy to supply vanadium electrolyte and battery stacks; Secures electrolyte lease for 48 MWh flow battery project in Texas, supported by Largo Physical Vanadium’s unique electrolyte leasing model."

Energy Fuels Inc. [TSX:EFR] (UUUU)

Energy Fuels states they are "the No. 1 uranium producer in the U.S. with a market-leading portfolio", as well as being a small vanadium producer.

No vanadium-related news for the month. There was some uranium & rare earths news.

Ferro-Alloy Resources [LON:FAR]

FAR is operating the giant Balasausqandiq vanadium project in Kyzylordinskaya Oblast of southern Kazakhstan. FAR stated in May 2025: "On a forward-looking basis, process plant operations will be conducted only when suitably profitable raw materials are available."

On June 27, Ferro-Alloy Resources announced:

Carbon Black Substitute product update and Feasibility Study update... Successful laboratory testing of the new CBS product (and the existing c. 40% carbon CBS product) by a major manufacturer and supplier of automotive rubber products to the Chinese car industry opens the prospect of a valuable CBS revenue stream with the potential to be brought online prior to the construction of the Project's main processing plant.

On July 7, Ferro-Alloy Resources announced:

Issue of Equity. Ferro-Alloy Resources Limited (LSE:FAR)...is pleased to announce that it has issued a total of 16,666,667 ordinary shares of nil par value in the capital of the Company ("New Shares"), raising gross proceeds of £1,000,000, having received share subscriptions from investors including certain directors of the Company and the Company's strategic shareholder Vision Blue Resources Limited ("VBR"). Commenting, Nick Bridgen, CEO of Ferro-Alloy Resources, said: "This small equity raise, supported by our key shareholders, will help us to optimise the carbon black substitute element of the Project and we look forward to the full results of the Feasibility Study in September."

Western Uranium & Vanadium Corp. (OTCQX:WSTRF)

Western Uranium & Vanadium Corp. own the Sunday Mine Complex, which is an advanced stage mine property consisting of five interconnected underground mines in Colorado, USA.

No significant news for the month.

VanadiumCorp Resources Inc. [TSXV:VRB][GR:NWN] (OTCPK:VRBFF)

VanadiumCorp Resources Inc. 100% owns the Lac Dore Vanadium-Iron-Titanium Project in Quebec, Canada. The Company is looking to take a vertically integrated approach and is also developing leading process technologies, "VanadiumCorp-Electrochem Processing Technology" and "Electrochem globally patented Electrowinning" technology.

On July 9, VanadiumCorp Resources Inc. announced: "VanadiumCorp announces $500,000 private placement financing."

On July 11, VanadiumCorp Resources Inc. announced: "VanadiumCorp closes first tranche of financing."

Vanadium developers

Neometals [ASX:NMT] (OTCQX:NMTAY)(OTCPK:RDRUY) (OTCPK:RRSSF)

Neometals 100% own the Barrambie Titanium Vanadium Iron Project in Western Australia, but are looking at divesting the asset. They also own 86.1% of the Vanadium Recovery Project JV.

On July 29, Neometals announced:

Quarterly activities report for the quarter ended 30 June 2025." Highlights include:

  • Discussions continued in relation to the divestment of the Barrambie titanium and vanadium deposit.
  • Vanadium Recovery Project (“VRP”) (86.1% NMT, via Recycling Industries Scandinavia AB (“RISAB”)). Equity and debt funding process continued with a number of parties in the project data room.
  • Cash balance of A$4.4M; investments and net receivables of A$11.0M and no debt.

Australian Vanadium [ASX:AVL] [GR:JT71]

Australian Vanadium is an emerging vanadium producer focused on their Australian Vanadium Project in Western Australia. VSUN Energy was launched by AVL in 2016 to target the energy storage market for vanadium redox flow batteries [VRFBs].

No news for the month.

Tivan Limited [ASX:TVN] (formerly TNG Ltd [ASX:TNG](OTCPK:TNGZF)

Tivan 100% owns Speewah Mining Pty Ltd, the owner of the Speewah Vanadium-Titanium-Iron Project and the Speewah Fluorite Project in WA. They also own the high grade Sandover Fluorite Project in Northern Territory.

On July 21, Tivan Limited announced:

Tivan and Sumitomo Corporation complete establishment of the Speewah Fluorite Project Joint Venture. Japan Organization for Metals and Energy Security agrees equity interest in Speewah...

On July 21, Tivan Limited announced: "Tivan and Sumitomo Corporation agree Memorandum of Understanding for Sandover Fluorite Project."

On July 24, Tivan Limited announced: "Tivan Limited - Quarterly activities report." Highlights include:

Highlights in the Quarter

  • "...Tivan raised $5 million through a share placement with the Australian family office investor...
  • A new Leadership Team was formed comprised of six senior managers to further Tivan’s operational capabilities and support the IJV with Sumitomo Corporation for the Speewah Fluorite Project.
  • In Timor-Leste, Tivan appointed a Country Representative, two local geologists and established a satellite office in Dili, in support of an extensive program of fieldwork at the Turiscai Project that has commenced.
  • Tivan completed full redemption of all of the convertible notes that had been issued..."

Vanadium Resources Limited [ASX:VR8] [GR:TR3]

Vanadium Resources is a junior exploration company established with the purpose of exploring and developing gold zinc, lead, copper and other mineral opportunities. Vanadium Resources owns 86.49% of a globally significant vanadium project, the Steelpoortdrift [SPD] Project, in Gauteng Province, South Africa.

On July 22, Vanadium Resources announced:

Binding Offtake for 100KTPM executed for DSO.

  • Vanadium Resources [PTY] Ltd (“VanRes”), a subsidiary of VR8, has signed a binding two-year offtake agreement with China Precious Asia Limited ("CPAL"), for the supply of vanadium-rich magnetite Direct Shipping Ore (“DSO Product”), from the world-class Steelpoortdrift Vanadium Project (“Project”) in South Africa.
  • Due to the absence of a standard spot price for this DSO Product, the Agreement is conditional on VanRes finalising pricing terms with CPAL by 30 August 2025. Additionally, as mining operations have yet to commence, the Agreement is subject to VanRes appointing a suitable mining contractor and ensuring that the DSO Product meets the agreed specifications (Collectively “Conditions Precedent”).

On July 25, Vanadium Resources announced: "Oversubscribed placement to advance near-term production. Firm commitments for A$1.2 million raised."

On July 28, Vanadium Resources announced: "Quarterly Activities/Appendix 5B Cash Flow Report...As at the end of June 2025 quarter, VR8 and its subsidiaries held total cash and cash equivalents of $160,087."

Richmond Vanadium Technology [ASX:RVT] ("RVT")

RVT now owns 100% of the Richmond Vanadium Project. It has a global Mineral Resource of 1.8Bt @ 0.36% Vanadium Pentoxide (V2O5).

On July 28, RVT announced:

Quarterly Activities Report for the period ending 30th June 2025. RVT has elected to slow certain activities associated with the Bankable Feasibility Study [BFS] and approvals process to conserve cash. Key growth initiatives RVT will now focus on include:

  • Completing additional metallurgical test work in 2025
  • Completing reserve drilling within current resource base to define a coarse ore higher grade mining inventory for initial development
  • Continue BFS activities focused on the mine development and concentrator with expected completion in the second half of 2026
  • Continuing the EIS approvals and Mining Lease application process in parallel with the BFS study
  • Minimise non-critical expenditure to conserve current cash reserves
  • Working with our partners Rongke Power and Trina Solar to install a demonstration VFB energy storage system in 2026...

Phenom Resources Corp. [TSXV:PHNM] (OTCQX:PHNMF) (formerly First Vanadium Corp.)

The Carlin Gold-Vanadium Property hosts one of North America’s largest richest primary vanadium deposits, located in Nevada. Its West Jerome project targets a large-scale high-grade copper and zinc deposit in Arizona. Carlin has a Historic Inferred Resource 28Mt at 0.525% V2O5 (2010 SRK).

No vanadium news, but there was some gold news.

CleanTech Vanadium Mining Corp. [TSXV:CTV] (OTCQB:CTVFF)

CleanTech Vanadium's flagship project is the Gibellini Vanadium Project in Nevada, USA. Gibellini holds 127 million pounds of measured and indicated V2O5, with 195 million pounds inferred. CleanTech aims to start the first North American vanadium mine. The Gibellini Vanadium Project has also shown potential for gold exploration.

No news for the month.

QEM Limited [ASX:QEM]

QEM Limited owns the Julia Creek Vanadium and Energy Project ("JCVEP") in North Western Queensland, Australia.

On July 2, QEM announced: "QEM appoints Robert Cooper as MD and CEO...effective 2 July 2025...Mr Cooper brings over 30 years of global mining experience."

Graphite miners with potential vanadium projects

  • Syrah Resources [ASX:SYR] (OTCPK:SYAAF) (OTC:SRHYY)
  • NextSource Materials [TSX:NEXT]
  • DNI Metals [TSXV:DNI] [GR:DG7N](OTC:DMNKF)

Other listed vanadium juniors

  • BlackRock Metals (Private)
  • Blue Sky Uranium [TSXV:BSK] (OTCQB:BKUCF)
  • Critical Minerals Group [ASX:CMG]
  • Currie Rose Resources Inc. [TSXV:CUI]
  • Gladiator Resources [ASX:GLA]
  • Golden Deeps [ASX:GED]
  • Intermin Resources [ASX:IRC]
  • Manuka Resources [ASX:MKR]
  • Maxtech Ventures [CSE:MVT]
  • Moab Minerals [ASX:MOM]
  • New Energy Minerals [ASX: NXE]
  • Pursuit Minerals [ASX:PUR]
  • Sabre Resources [ASX:SBR]
  • Santa Fe Minerals [ASX:SFM]
  • Strategic Resources [TSXV:SR] (OTCPK:SCCFF)
  • Surefire Resources [ASX:SRN]
  • Trigon Metals Inc. [TSXV:TM] (OTC:PNTZF)
  • UVRE [ASX:UVA]
  • Venus Metals [ASX:VMC]
  • Victory Metals [TSXV:VMX]
  • Viking Mines [ASX:VKA]

VRFB Companies

  • Protean Energy [ASX:POW] [GR:SHE1]
  • Enerox GmbH (90% Bushveld/10% Cellcube Energy Storage Systems)
  • Invinity Energy Systems (LSE:IES) (IVVGF) (OTCQX:IESVF)

Conclusion

July saw Europe & China spot vanadium pentoxide prices and ferrovanadium prices lower for the past month. Prices remain at very low levels.

Highlights for the month include:

  • VRFB demand forecast to account for 17% of global vanadium use — a x6 increase from just 3% in 2021.
  • Vanitec reported on new and completed VRFB and hybrid (Lithium-ion, VRFB, Molten Salt Thermal Storage) projects in China and Mongolia.
  • Glencore announces US$1 billion 2025/2026 share buy-back programme.
  • Largo Inc. operational turnaround progressing as planned, on track to achieve 2025 V₂O₅ production guidance.
  • Ferro-Alloy Resources - Carbon Black Substitute product successful laboratory testing by a major manufacturer and supplier of automotive rubber opens the prospect of a valuable CBS revenue stream to be brought online prior to the construction of the Project's main processing plant.
  • Vanadium Resources has signed a binding two-year offtake agreement with China Precious Asia for the supply of vanadium-rich magnetite Direct Shipping Ore from their Steelpoortdrift Vanadium Project in South Africa.

As usual, all comments are welcome.

data.title: German Inflation Unchanged At 2% In July
data.description:
By Carsten Brzeski

The just-released flash estimate of German inflation for July shows that the disinflationary process continues in baby steps. Headline inflation, according to the national measure, remained unchanged at 2% year-on-year, while the European measure dropped to 1.8% YoY, from 2.0% YoY in June. Core inflation remained unchanged at 2.7% YoY.

German inflation to remain 'below but close to 2%'

The available regional state data confirms that Germany, along with the broader eurozone economy, is currently experiencing a process of disinflation, not deflation, as actually only monthly prices for clothing and heating oil fell. Almost all other categories saw prices increasing, albeit often at a slower pace than last month.

Looking ahead, at least in the nearer term, German inflation is likely to fulfil the ECB’s old definition of price stability and should remain ‘below but close to 2%’. Favourable energy base effects are petering out, while core inflation should be coming down somewhat. It remains to be seen how European and US companies will react to US tariffs. While one scenario could see prices falling in the eurozone due to overcapacities and weaker sales in the US, globally operating companies might try to actually increase prices in Europe in order to offset profit squeezing in the US. A rather domestic theme will be the cooling of the German labour market, which should take away wage pressures and consequently inflationary pressures. On the other hand, the government’s fiscal stimulus is likely to push up inflationary pressure towards the end of the year and beyond.

The latest trade developments and the avoidance of a full-blown trade conflict have alleviated pressure on the ECB to continue cutting rates to support eurozone growth. Add to this the latest weakening of the euro as well as meagre but positive GDP growth in the second quarter and the bar for yet another rate cut this year has clearly risen. Still, at least for the next few weeks, we will stick to our call of one final rate cut at the September meeting. Why? First of all, a deal is only a deal once it’s signed on paper. This particularly applies to trade negotiations these days and it wouldn’t be the first time that some last-minute announcements or disruptions occur. There is also our view that both actual inflation and hard macro data could come in weaker than softer data over the coming weeks, fueling the inflation undershooting narrative. Finally, and the strongest argument, is the fact that the ECB’s June projections actually included a terminal rate of 1.75%. Not delivering on these market prices would mechanically push down the ECB’s own inflation and growth projections.

Admittedly, with recent developments, the degree of conviction for our own call has also come down. In fact, if the easing of trade tensions lifts uncertainty and increases resilience of the eurozone economy, the debate at the ECB could quickly shift; from whether or not more rate cuts are needed to when to hike rates to tackle inflationary pressures stemming from fiscal stimulus.

Content Disclaimer

This publication has been prepared by ING solely for information purposes irrespective of a particular user's means, financial situation or investment objectives. The information does not constitute investment recommendation, and nor is it investment, legal or tax advice or an offer or solicitation to purchase or sell any financial instrument. Read more

Original Post

data.title: Dream Finders Homes: Land-Light Model Fairly Valued
data.description:
Dream Finders Homes, Inc. (NYSE:DFH) has had a tough year in terms of stock price being down significantly. The company has generated positive net income for the last 5 years and has been in growth mode, with 2019 income at $39 million and the trailing 12-month at $339 million.

Homebuilders usually don't have extreme competitive advantages, but some attributes can be fostered to drive good economic returns. The homebuilder business model structure matters as it can be capital-intensive. As long-term investors, this is exactly what we don't want. DFH has fostered a different route to home building by using a land-light business model.

Avoiding Large Land Investments

A typical homebuilder owns the land, develops the land, and builds on spec or sells before building. This whole scenario makes the builder own a large piece of land, which is a big investment and ties up capital. It can also go really south for the companies that own the land, as the land could decline in value, and now their capital is tied up in an asset that has to be marked down.

DFH has chosen a different path, using an outsourced third party to own and process the land and get it ready to build on. This allows DFH to only purchase the land when it's ready to build on and protects itself from adverse market forces.

The model has really changed the game, allowing hundreds of millions of capital being freed up instead of being stashed in land investments. It's difficult for competitors to change course also, as large investments in land banks already sit on their books, so pivoting to the model takes time.

Management wanted a more nimble/flexible approach while de-risking the balance sheet. The slide below really highlights why management went with this strategy:

The Business Model Works

NVR (NVR) was one of the first to use the land-light model and has proven it works by generating high returns on equity for decades. In addition, the stock price has provided substantial returns to investors.

DFH is copying NVR's strategy, and their numbers prove the strategy is working, as they've consistently produced a 35% plus return on equity over the years.

Alignment with Investors

Patrick Zalupski's is the type of CEO you want running your business. Provides good insights into the business through annual shareholder letters and owns 60% plus of the business. I repeat, he owns 60% plus of the business and is the majority of his net worth. With such a large stake, it's natural for the captain of the ship to think long-term and navigate the business through rough seas. Historically, the leadership of the company has made sound decisions with capital allocation. Capital allocation is such an imperative part of growing a business, especially in the homebuilding space. Patrick's track record so far has been strong, indicating superior management capabilities.

Historically, the company had to lever up substantially in order to make as many acquisitions as they've made. These investments are important for growth as homebuilders need to secure finished lots and penetrate new markets. Once things are running smoothly and cash flow starts to be generated from the investments, debt paydown can occur along with returning capital to shareholders.

Management has done a good job with this cycle.

In 2020 and 2021, the company was highly levered and has been able to reduce its ratios from 167% to 91% in 5 years. EBITDA has also compounded substantially with the acquisitions, making it a more favorable debt/EBITDA ratio.

Valuation

High interest rates and a weaker market have really put strain on the stock prices of builders over the last 12 months. Dream Finders Homes is no exception to this.

At one point, shares were trading as high as $39 a share, and lately, it has been hovering between $20 and $27 a share. Earnings per share are around $3 per share, giving an 11% earnings yield at the current market price. Context is key here, as an 11% yield isn't unusual at times in an industry with such variable dynamics. From a peer analysis, it looks like we get a little discount on the earnings yield against peers.

With home builders, buying at favorable valuations really can protect us from the downsize, as adverse market dynamics do occur in the home building market. Earnings can be variable because of factors out of management's control. Interest rate and household income play a huge factor on the demand side of this industry. As investors, we need to protect ourselves by purchasing shares cheaply, and I'm not seeing that protection with Dream Finders Homes yet. I would like to see the company continue to drive positive earnings and start returning a little more to the buyback program.

Risks

Interest rates and the concentration of where they operate are the two factors I'm looking at closely. Interest rates continue to be elevated making it much more difficult for homebuyers.

There hasn't been much relief from the high rates over the last 6 months, as you can see from the graph above, staying in the high 6% on the 30-year mortgage.

Operation concentration is also a risk, as Dream Finders Homes has benefited over the years with COVID, as people were moving to the southeast and Texas. Only time will tell how they will do in more of a normal market cycle.

Conclusion

Be patient with this company, as I do believe it is one to have on the watchlist. I like the land-light business model, owner operator CEO with massive skin in the game, and already generating some favorable metrics in a very difficult industry. The macroeconomic situation isn't favorable, which is driving my decision to be cautious and wait patiently for a better entry.

data.title: British American Tobacco H1: Stock Price Up, Vuse Disappoints, I Sell Some Shares
data.description:
British American Tobacco p.l.c. (NYSE:BTI, LSE: BATS) is one of the dividend-paying companies with the greatest weight in my portfolio. For years, I have always considered it a buy or strong buy, but after the recent rise and the H1 2025 results, I decided to sell some shares at a profit.

As a result, I believe that today it is more appropriate to rate it as a Hold. Let me explain why in detail.

Highlights H1 2025

Before commenting on the results for H1 2025, I think it is worth highlighting BTI's performance since the beginning of the year as it has been impressive.

A +46% price return that does not take into account the huge dividend, a gain far greater than what you would have earned by investing in the S&P 500. As much as I appreciate BTI, I believe that what has happened is quite anomalous; after all, we are talking about a company with low growth or even negative growth in some cases.

The challenge of increasing cash from operations has always been there given the struggles of the industry it operates in, but in January the stock was so undervalued that even with low-single digit growth there was a good chance of achieving significant returns (which is why I gave it a buy rating).

This was indeed the case, but today we are in a different situation. As we will see, BTI continues to have growth problems, but its valuation multiples are no longer the same as in previous months.

Let's be clear, the multiple is not in a bubble (otherwise I would not have sold just a few shares), but I believe it has expanded in a way that is not in line with fundamentals. I would have justified a higher multiple in light of significantly improving fundamentals, but that is not what I saw in H1 2025.

Compared to H1 2024, revenues in £ fell by 2.20%, but that's not what worries me so much. We all know that the tobacco industry is bound to decline gradually over time, and the goal of these companies is to steer consumers toward less harmful alternatives. Yet revenue from new categories has remained flat. Frankly, I expected better results, especially since competitors such as Philip Morris (PM) are already much less dependent on the sale of combustible tobacco products.

Philip Morris' smoke-free products are growing at double digits despite accounting for almost a third of total sales. According to BTI, they account for only 18.20% of sales, and compared to H1 2024, there has been an improvement of just 70 basis points. In my opinion, the company is taking too long to transition from combustible products to smoke-free products.

Total smokeless products (New Categories + Traditional Oral) in the US saw sales decline by 0.80%: we are off track if the goal is to replace cigarettes. Of course, Modern Oral grew at an impressive rate of 372% following the launch of Velo Plus, but this segment represents only 10% of the Total Smokeless segment.

Vapor, through the Vuse brand, is by far the most important market for BTI, accounting for 41% of the Total Smokeless segment, and has seen sales decline by as much as 13.80%. In effect, it has wiped out the progress made in Modern Oral.

The company continues to blame the proliferation of illicit disposable vapes in the US, the world's largest vapor market. According to BTI estimates, over 50% of it is based on illicit vapes, and solving this problem would give a huge boost to its sales.

However, the months are passing and I see no improvement in this regard. Let me be clear, this is not BTI's fault, but we cannot assume that this problem will suddenly disappear. The company has several new products in the pipeline to attract new customers, including Vuse Ultra, and I hope it succeeds, although I am slightly pessimistic about this.

Our new premium innovation, Vuse Ultra, offers consumers a highly differentiated, connected and customisable experience. We are encouraged by the early performance in Canada and will continue the roll-out in a targeted way through H2 2025.

All in all, compared to a company like Philip Morris, I believe that BTI is lagging far behind in terms of product innovation. The results mentioned above are those obtained in the US, but the situation is no better in other geographical areas.

BTI is still too dependent on combustible tobacco, and this factor, combined with the rapid increase in the share price, led me to sell some shares at a significant profit.

The reason I did not sell my entire position is that I still believe in BTI's potential for double-digit growth in smokeless products, and management is more optimistic about the second half of the year. In addition, the company is experiencing significant improvements in debt sustainability, an issue that scared investors until a few years ago.

Net debt, although still high, fell by 10% compared to last year and there are no significant debt maturities in the next 12 months. From this point of view, the gradual sale of the stake in ITC is helping a lot.

These inflows of billions of pounds are making BTI's financial structure much stronger, and I expect this trend to continue. Consider that in H1, proceeds from the sale of ITC shares were £904 million, and BTI's ownership fell by 2.50%. However, as of June 30, 2025, it still stands at 22.93%, so there is still plenty of room to unlock further billions.

The knowledge that it can quickly tap into such large sums is probably why BTI increased its 2025 share repurchase by £200 million to £1.10 billion. Shareholders always like buybacks, but I invite you to consider an alternative point of view.

Buybacks are most effective when the share price is low, so it would have made much more sense to increase them when the stock was falling. Doing buybacks at the wrong time when your debt is still quite high can backfire. Management probably thinks that BTI shares are still undervalued, which would justify the buyback, but we'll see. Personally, I would have preferred that this £1.10 billion be used to invest in smokeless products or further reduce debt, but that is just my opinion.

Finally, in addition to the stake in ITC, the other reason that leads me to hold BTI is the sustainability of its dividend despite its very high; the cash dividend payout ratio is only 54%. Those who buy it now get 6%, while those who bought it last year may have achieved a double-digit dividend on cost.

Conclusion

Overall, BTI remains a solid company with a significant stake in the ITC giant and increasingly sustainable debt. Dividends and buybacks make it a highly attractive company for those seeking a passive income that is not too risky.

The tobacco industry is in decline, as we all know, but it still has a long way to go before it ceases to exist. Consider that BTI's revenues from combustible products in the US recorded sales growth of 1.10% in H1 2025, so there are still years, if not decades, to exploit this market.

My only concern stems from the slow pace of improvement in the smokeless segment, which should be growing the most in this transition phase (Philip Morris is well ahead). Vuse is disappointing, and even though modern oral is growing rapidly, it has not managed to make the overall result less bitter. This factor, combined with the rapid growth in the share price in recent months, has led me to sell some shares and remain vigilant about Vuse's growth in H2. Management has promised a recovery, so we'll see.

Editor's Note: This article discusses one or more securities that do not trade on a major U.S. exchange. Please be aware of the risks associated with these stocks.

data.title: lululemon: A Quality Compounder Trading At Discounted Multiples
data.description:
Investment Thesis

I believe lululemon athletica inc. (NASDAQ:LULU) has the hallmarks of a typical quality company. It has ample reinvestment opportunities, a high-level ROIC, a healthy balance sheet, an ability to generate high free cash flows and a favorable shareholder return policy. However, over the past period, the company's fundamentals have strengthened, but its market valuation remains near its lowest levels. I believe this is a good chance for public investors who want to invest in a quality stock at a discounted price.

In this article, I talk about lululemon's recent results and why it is a great quality company. And I estimated the expected return if the market fairly re-evaluates its value.

Company Snapshot

lululemon is a premium athletic clothing company best known for yoga and fitness wear. In 2024, it reported $10.6 billion in revenue, up 10% from the prior year. Growth has slowed compared to past years, but over the last five years, the company has posted a significant growth rate of 22%, which is much faster than its peers.

lululemon runs its business in three main regions: the Americas, Mainland China, and the Rest of the World. In 2024, the Americas made up 75% of total revenue, growing only 4% from last year. This slower growth made some investors worry about the U.S. market. Mainland China brought in 13% of revenue and grew 41%, becoming the company’s main growth driver. The Rest of World segment had 12% of revenue and grew 27%.

From its latest earnings call, lululemon kept its full-year sales growth target at 7–8%. The company said it may also raise prices to handle tariffs, which are expected to hurt most in Q2. I think the impact will be short-term. With a gross margin of nearly 60%, the company shows it has strong pricing power and a brand that customers value.

Why It Is a Great Quality Company

The typical logic behind quality investing is to invest in companies that are financially sound and have a long-term competitive advantage, thereby pursuing long-term compounding effects. For this to be realized, a company's business must generate a return higher than its cost of capital and there must be ample opportunities to reinvest that business.

Typically, a company's competitive advantage can be quantitatively measured through its ROIC calculation. lululemon has demonstrated an excellent and sustainably high ROIC of above 40%.

[ROIC Calculation, Unit: $ Millions]

Jan-21

Jan-22

Jan-23

Jan-24

Feb-25
EBIT 813 1,375 1,726 2,211 2,506
Tax expenses 230 359 478 626 762
Operating profits after tax 582 1,016 1,248 1,585 1,744
Cash 1,151 1,260 1,155 2,244 1,984
Total Debt 799 881 1,070 1,403 1,576
Total Equity 2,559 2,740 3,149 4,232 4,324

Invested capital

2,207

2,361

3,064

3,391

3,916
ROIC 44.5% 46.0% 49.1% 47.7%

lululemon's ability to consistently achieve such high ROIC is due to its strong brand and pricing power. Brand is important. The stronger the brand, the greater the customer loyalty, leading to repeat purchases and greater pricing power.

lululemon's key differentiating strategy is community-based marketing. Unlike traditional sports brands, which often employ famous athletes or celebrities, lululemon appoints local yoga instructors, Pilates instructors, and running coaches as ambassadors. They hold classes in lululemon's stores, naturally fostering an emotional connection between the brand and consumers. lululemon shifted the in-store experience from a simple purchase to an education-focused experience. Limited price discounts and promotions help stimulate a value-driven consumer mentality.

Thanks to its differentiation strategy, lululemon maintains a gross profit margin in the high 50% range, which proves it enjoys a price premium of about 20% compared to the sector median of 38%. And, the inventory level is consistently maintained at around 15% of sales, which appears to maintain high levels of customer loyalty and repeat purchases.

[Gross profit margins and Inventory levels Calculation, Unit: $ Millions]

Jan-21

Jan-22

Jan-23

Jan-24

Feb-25
Revenue 4,402 6,257 8,111 9,619 10,588
Cost of goods sold 1,938 2,648 3,618 4,010 4,317

Gross Profit

 2,464 3,609 4,492 5,609 6,271

Gross Profit Margins

56%

58%

55%

58%

 59%

Inventory

 647 1,009 1,447 1,324 1,442
% Inventory 15% 16% 18% 14% 14%

Next, let's examine the company's growth potential. As mentioned earlier, the company's current largest market is the Americas, where approximately 75% of its sales are generated. In 2024, the Americas segment reported approximately $790 million in sales, a mere 3.9% year-over-year growth. Despite the slowdown in the US market, considering the overall sales growth of 10%, I believe the company has ample reinvestment opportunities in markets outside the US.

China, in particular, presents a wealth of growth opportunities. Sales in China reached approximately $1,361 million in 2024, a 41.3% year-on-year increase. The number of stores in China reached 151, representing 20% of the company's 767 total stores and an 18.9% increase from 127 the previous year, demonstrating the strongest growth among all regions. This increase in store count will likely serve as a foundation for future profit growth and contribute to sustained growth.

Even compared to the great sports brand Nike. In 2024, Nike's sales in China reported $7,545 million, a 4% year-over-year increase. This figure is approximately 5.5 times larger than lululemon's sales in China. Assuming lululemon continues to grow at a 20% annual rate in the Chinese market, this suggests reinvestment opportunities for the next nine to ten years.

In short, economic moats such as a strong brand and long-term growth opportunities provide the perfect combination for investors seeking compounding benefits. Let's also touch on a few other quality factors.

A stable financial structure is important to maximize long-term compounding effects because a highly leveraged financial structure makes it difficult for a company to withstand unexpected economic cycles. In 2024, lululemon has approximately $1,500 million in debt and $1,900 million in cash, meaning it has negative net debt.

And lululemon's CAPEX over the past five years is approximately $500 million, and its leverage calculated by excluding CAPEX from EBITDA is also less than 1x, so lululemon's financial leverage is very low and it possesses high financial flexibility.

[Leverage ratio, Unit: $ Millions]

Total Debt 799 881 1,070 1,403 1,576

EBITDA

 998 1,599 2,018 2,590 2,952

Leverage (x)

0.8

0.6

0.5

0.5

0.5
Capex (229) (395) (639) (652) (689)

EBITDAX

769

1,205

1,379

1,938

2,263

Leverage (x)

1.04

0.73

0.78

0.72

0.70

Finally, no matter how much money a company earns, if it fails to use it to increase shareholder value, it will inevitably be undervalued by the market. The most common ways a company returns value to shareholders are dividends and share repurchases. lululemon primarily returns value to shareholders through share repurchases.

Over the past five years, lululemon has returned approximately $740 million to shareholders in the form of stock repurchases. The amount of stock repurchases continues to increase. The size of share repurchases, which was only 12% of operating cash flow five years ago, increased to 73% in 2024, and on average over the five years, approximately 45% of the money earned from operations was returned to shareholders. Considering that capex investment is approximately 36% of operating cash flow, there is room to increase share repurchases by approximately 20% going forward.

Valuation

Quality investing focuses on maximizing compounding. To capitalize on this benefit, long-term investments are preferred over short-term ones. Therefore, for valuation purposes, I estimated future cash flows for at least five years and calculated the expected investment return.

First, I assumed different sales growth rates for each market due to market opportunities. With annual growth of 4% in the US, 20% in China, and 15% in other markets, a company-wide sales growth rates are expected to be approximately 8% for the next five years.

[Revenue Forecasts, Unit: $ Millions]

Feb-25 FY1 FY2 FY3 FY4 FY5
Americas 7,928 8,245 8,575 8,918 9,275 9,646
China Mainland 1,361 1,634 1,960 2,352 2,823 3,387
Rest of World 1,299 1,493 1,717 1,975 2,271 2,612
Total Revenue 10,588 11,372 12,253 13,246 14,369 15,645

Next, I forecasted its profitability for the next five years. Considering that lululemon's competitive advantage, brand power, has been maintained stably over the past period, I believe that its economic moat will remain strong and will be able to maintain the company's profitability in the future. Therefore, I assume its gross profit margin and operating margin will remain at their five-year average going forward.

[Margin Forecasts, Unit: $ Millions]

Feb-25 FY1 FY2 FY3 FY4 FY5
Total Revenue 10,588 11,372 12,253 13,246 14,369 15,645
Gross Profit 6,271 6,518 7,023 7,592 8,236 8,967
Gross Profit Margins 59% 57% 57% 57% 57% 57%
SG&A 3,762 4,043 4,356 4,709 5,109 5,563
% of Revenue 36% 36% 36% 36% 36% 36%
EBIT 2,508 2,475 2,666 2,883 3,127 3,405
EBIT Margins 24% 22% 22% 22% 22% 22%
Depreciation 444 435 468 506 549 598
% of Revenue 4% 4% 4% 4% 4% 4%
EBITDA 2,952 2,910 3,135 3,389 3,676 4,003
EBITDA Margins 28% 26% 26% 26% 26% 26%

The last is to forecast free cash flows. FCF is calculated by subtracting capex, working capital investments, and taxes from EBITDA. And then, I assume that 50% of FCF is used for share repurchases. To calculate FCF before share repurchase, I used the 5-year average of each item. And I assume that 50% of the FCF calculated this way is used for share repurchases, with the remaining 50% retained as a cash balance.

[Cash Flow Projection, Unit: $ Millions]

Feb-25 FY1 FY2 FY3 FY4 FY5
EBITDA 3,396 3,344 3,603 3,895 4,225 4,601
CAPEX (689) (743) (801) (866) (939) (1022)
Change in WC (212) (276) (297) (321) (348) (379)
Cash Income Tax paid (579) (687) (740) (800) (868) (945)
FCF before buybacks 1916 1638 1765 1908 2070 2254
Share Repurchase (1672) (819) (883) (954) (1035) (1127)
FCF after buybacks 244 819 883 954 1035 1127
Cash Balance 1,984 2,803 3,686 4,640 5,675 6,802

The company's current market valuation is around 8–9x EV/EBITDA. The sector median is 10x, and lululemon's five-year average trading multiple was 26x. In other words, lululemon is trading at a significant undervaluation compared to its peer group and historical levels.

However, I view lululemon not simply as a retailer, but as a quality company. I frequently compare it to the Dow Jones Industrial Average's 30 non-financial companies as a comparable benchmark for quality companies. These companies have long operating records across various industries and hold leading positions in their industries, making them worthy of comparison as quality companies.

These companies are currently trading at an average EV/EBITDA multiple of approximately 16x. Given their average sales growth rate of 2.8%, EBITDA margin of 25%, and return on capital of 11.1%, I see no reason for lululemon to be valued any lower than these companies. Therefore, I believe lululemon deserves a market valuation of up to 15x over the long term.

[List of Comparable Quality Companies]

Symbol Name EV / EBITDA Revenue YoY EBITDA Margin Return on Total Capital
AAPL Apple Inc. 22.47 4.91% 34.68% 46.31%
AMGN Amgen Inc. 13.47 15.56% 46.08% 9.67%
BA The Boeing Company NM 2.41% -8.88% -11.61%
CAT Caterpillar Inc. 16.10 -5.58% 23.38% 14.14%
CRM Salesforce, Inc. 22.33 7.97% 29.04% 6.80%
CSCO Cisco Systems, Inc. 19.23 0.47% 26.50% 9.67%
CVX Chevron Corporation 9.02 1.19% 19.73% 7.46%
DIS The Walt Disney Company 13.44 5.42% 20.33% 5.81%
DOW Dow Inc. 8.95 -2.82% 9.24% 1.70%
HD The Home Depot, Inc. 17.10 7.33% 15.65% 21.80%
HON Honeywell International Inc. 16.90 7.11% 24.90% 10.47%
IBM International Business Machines Corporation 20.93 2.69% 22.15% 7.58%
INTC Intel Corporation 13.73 -3.72% 17.34% -1.03%
JNJ Johnson & Johnson 14.44 4.68% 33.38% 11.76%
KO The Coca-Cola Company 21.30 1.28% 33.53% 12.01%
MCD McDonald's Corporation 19.28 -0.23% 54.06% 15.44%
MMM 3M Company 15.61 0.24% 23.55% 16.59%
MRK Merck & Co., Inc. 8.00 1.82% 45.83% 18.81%
MSFT Microsoft Corporation 25.71 14.13% 55.25% 19.42%
NKE NIKE, Inc. 26.26 -9.84% 9.67% 9.14%
PG The Procter & Gamble Company 16.83 0.29% 27.64% 14.94%
VZ Verizon Communications Inc. 7.08 2.05% 36.34% 7.12%
WBA Walgreens Boots Alliance, Inc. 13.00 6.22% 1.96% 1.46%
WMT Walmart Inc. 19.90 4.22% 6.24% 11.75%
AVERAGE 16.57 2.8% 25.3% 11.1%

Applying a multiple of 15x to lululemon's five-year projected EBITDA yields an EV of approximately $60 billion. To get the expected equity value, I assume the debt remains at the current level and the cash balance would accumulate with annual free cash flows. Then, the equity value is expected to be around $65 billion. With a current market cap of $25 billion, this represents approximately 2.5x growth and an IRR of approximately 20%.

In short, buying a good business at a low multiple and waiting for the market to revalue it is a solid investment idea.

Conclusion

lululemon has many traits of a quality company. It has a strong brand, healthy cash flow, low debt, and a favorable shareholder return policy. However, sometimes the market misses good investment opportunities or overreacts to temporary factors, resulting in good stocks being undervalued.

I believe lululemon is a strong company that the market is ignoring. Over time, I think it will get the revaluation it deserves and give a huge return to investors who move proactively.

data.title: Honeywell: Price Pullback Is A Great Opportunity
data.description:
Hunting for stock bargains can be a highly rewarding endeavor. However, looking only at stocks with low PE ratios can cause one to miss out on some great long-term wealth compounders. These are what I would call 'set it and forget it' types of stocks that ought to be accumulated when they are reasonably priced. That's because these companies have moat-worthy businesses that rarely, if ever, result in a 'cheap' valuation. Bottom of Form

Such I find the case to be with Honeywell International (NASDAQ:HON), which I last covered back in April, highlighting an opportune time to buy the stock, its focus on energy security and digitalization, and long-term growth prospects in aerospace and automation. That turned out to be good timing, as HON has delivered a 19% total return for investors since my last piece.

The total return would have been higher had it not been for the recent dip in price from $240 to $222. While HON isn't cheap, it's far from being expensive, either, with a forward PE of 21. In this article, I revisit HON, including recent business fundamentals, and discuss why the recent pullback in price presents a solid entry point for this GARP (growth at a reasonable price) stock, so let's get started!

Why HON?

Honeywell International is a diversified industrial conglomerate. It operates in four key segments of Aerospace Technologies (commercial, defense, and space), Industrial Automation (process solutions, warehouse automations, sensing technologies), Building Automation (HVAC, energy management), and Energy & Sustainability (advanced materials, sustainable fuels).

Notably, management plans to unlock shareholder value, similar to what industrial peer GE (GE) did, by spinning off its Advanced Materials and Aerospace segments in two separate transactions over the next 12 months. This creates 3 distinct entities and enables RemainCo to focus on its Automation core competency across the Industrial and Building sectors.

This could help HON to create shareholder value, as the three standalone businesses could be worth more than the sum of the parts. As shown below, HON had a decent track record of beating the S&P 500 (SPY) in total return over the past 10 years until 2023, when it started to lag the overall market.

Honeywell demonstrated respectable results during Q2 2025, with organic sales rising by 5% YoY, which was above the high end of guidance range. This was led by robust growth in Building Automation, which saw its third consecutive quarter of 8% sales growth. As shown below, all segments except for Industrial Automation saw growth in the 6–8% range during the second quarter.

More encouraging is HON's higher profit growth (compared to revenue growth) of 8% YoY, due to segment margin expansion in the faster growing Building and Industrial Automation segments. This resulted in robust adjusted EPS growth of 10% YoY, exceeding the upper end of management's guidance by 5 cents. Notably, HON increased R&D spend in all business segments, with it rising to 4.6% of sales. This upfront investment is designed to accelerate innovation-led growth in future periods.

Management raised its full-year revenue growth guidance from 3% previously to 4.5% at the midpoint of range and adjusted EPS growth of 7% to $10.55 at the midpoint of range. This is driven by management's expectations for segment margin growth of 50 basis points over the prior year. Despite higher tariffs and ongoing macroeconomic uncertainties, HON expects pricing productivity and alternative sourcing strategies to mitigate those impacts.

Near-term growth is supported by bolt-on acquisitions like the $1.8 billion purchase of Johnson Matthey's Catalyst Technologies business to enhance the UOP segment (energy solutions business). This transaction gives HON additional capabilities in sustainable methanol, sustainable aviation fuel, hydrogen, and ammonia to serve its customer base.

Another acquisition is Li-ion Tamer, which enhances HON's building automation capabilities in the high-growth energy and data center markets. Building automation has a sizable $100+ billion addressable market globally, and that's expected to grow at a robust 8.8% CAGR between now and the end of this decade. Additionally, the Industrial Automation market is expected to grow at a 9% CAGR through 2032 to $493 billion, as shown below.

HON is supported by a strong balance sheet with an 'A' credit rating from S&P. It has a very high cash and short-term investment balance of $10.7 billion and a safe net debt-to-TTM EBITDA ratio of 2.7x. The stock currently yields 2%, and the dividend is well covered by a 43% payout ratio. It also comes with a five-year dividend CAGR of 4.9% and 5 consecutive years of growth.

Admittedly, HON's yield isn't high, but it also returns capital to shareholders through stock buybacks, making it a total return play. As shown below, HON has reduced nearly 10% of its outstanding float over the past 5 years.

Lastly, while HON isn't cheap, it's far from being expensive, either, at the current price of $222 with a forward PE of 21.0. This is considering potential value creation opportunities from the planned spinoff of the Advanced Materials business later this year and the Aerospace spin-off planned for the second half of next year.

If the success of the GE spin-offs is of any indication, investors could see meaningful gains given the unique growth trajectories of each business. In particular, HON's Aerospace division provides technologies across a spectrum of commercial, business jets, and defense applications, making it a very well-rounded business. Based on my expectations for ~10% EPS growth annually on a combined basis due to the aforementioned catalysts, the dividend, and share buybacks, HON could produce potential double-digit total returns from here.

HON could see cost inflation from tariffs. While management appears to have a cost mitigation plan in place to counter tariffs, uncertainties in long-term tariff rates could result in margin compression. Recession risks in Europe and China could hurt short-cycle businesses like Sensing and Thermal Solutions. Lastly, spin-offs and potential for changes in valuations of these businesses are near-term risks.

Investor Takeaway

Honeywell is a solid opportunity for long-term investors seeking growth at a reasonable price. The stock remains attractively valued, especially after the recent pullback, given its strong fundamentals and strategic plans to unlock shareholder value through upcoming spin-offs of its Aerospace and Advanced Materials businesses.

HON's robust Q2 performance, increased R&D investment, and bolt-on acquisitions in high-growth sectors like sustainable fuels and building automation position it for continued earnings momentum. Honeywell is also backed by a solid balance sheet, disciplined capital returns, and exposure to secular growth markets. As such, HON presents a GARP opportunity with the potential for strong total returns over the coming years.
